meta.title: Boolean Expressions
meta.intro: You warmed these up on paper in Trace the Gate and Write the Rule — evaluating comparisons and combining them with NOT, AND, and OR at The Gauntlet's entry gate. Now type and run them for real, hardest last: the first prints a single height comparison, and the last builds the full boarding rule from scratch. Predict each True/False result first, then check it. Python prints Booleans as True and False.

problems.prompt: The Gauntlet requires riders to be at least 48 inches tall. With height set to 50, print the Boolean value of the comparison height ≥ 48 (should be True).
problems.promptHtml: The Gauntlet requires riders to be at least 48 inches tall. With height = 50, print the Boolean value of height ≥ 48 (should be True).

starter.python:
height = 50
# 'at least 48' includes 48 itself — use >= (not >)
# print the Boolean value of the comparison

problems.hint: 'At least 48' means 48 counts, so use >= , not > . The comparison height >= 48 evaluates to a Boolean; print it directly.

problems.prompt: A rider may board only if they are tall enough AND they hold a ticket. With height = 50 and hasTicket = False, print the Boolean value of (height ≥ 48) AND hasTicket (should be False).
problems.promptHtml: A rider may board only if they are tall enough AND they hold a ticket. With height = 50 and hasTicket = False, print the Boolean value of (height ≥ 48) AND hasTicket (should be False).

starter.python:
height = 50
has_ticket = False
# AND is true only when BOTH operands are true
# print the Boolean value of (height >= 48) and has_ticket

reference.pseudocode:
height ← 50
hasTicket ← false
DISPLAY((height ≥ 48) AND hasTicket)
reference.python:
height = 50
has_ticket = False
print(height >= 48 and has_ticket)

problems.hint: height >= 48 is True, but has_ticket is False. AND needs BOTH true, so True AND False is False. Use 'and' in Python.

problems.prompt: The express lane opens to a VIP OR a fast-pass holder, as long as the ride is NOT closed. With isVIP = False, hasFastPass = True, rideClosed = False, print the value of (isVIP OR hasFastPass) AND (NOT rideClosed) (should be True).
problems.promptHtml: The express lane opens to a VIP OR a fast-pass holder, as long as the ride is NOT closed. With isVIP = False, hasFastPass = True, rideClosed = False, print the value of (isVIP OR hasFastPass) AND (NOT rideClosed) (should be True).

starter.python:
is_vip = False
has_fast_pass = True
ride_closed = False
# work inside-out: OR is inclusive, NOT flips its operand
# print the Boolean value of the whole expression

reference.pseudocode:
isVIP ← false
hasFastPass ← true
rideClosed ← false
DISPLAY((isVIP OR hasFastPass) AND (NOT rideClosed))
reference.python:
is_vip = False
has_fast_pass = True
ride_closed = False
print((is_vip or has_fast_pass) and (not ride_closed))

problems.hint: Inside-out: (False OR True) is True because OR needs just one; (NOT False) is True; True AND True is True. Use or, and, not in Python.

problems.prompt: Write the full boarding rule from scratch. A guest boards when they are at least 48 inches tall, they hold a ticket, and the ride is open. Using the given values (height = 46, hasTicket = True, rideOpen = True), print the Boolean value of the complete rule (should be False).
problems.promptHtml: Write the full boarding rule from scratch. A guest boards when they are at least 48 inches tall, they hold a ticket, and the ride is open. Using the given values (height = 46, hasTicket = True, rideOpen = True), print the Boolean value of the complete rule (should be False).

starter.python:
height = 46
has_ticket = True
ride_open = True
# board when: tall enough (>= 48) AND has a ticket AND the ride is open
# build that whole Boolean expression and print it

reference.pseudocode:
height ← 46
hasTicket ← true
rideOpen ← true
DISPLAY(height ≥ 48 AND hasTicket AND rideOpen)
reference.python:
height = 46
has_ticket = True
ride_open = True
print(height >= 48 and has_ticket and ride_open)

problems.hint: Chain the three conditions with AND: height >= 48, has_ticket, ride_open. Height is 46, which is below 48, so the first operand is False — and one False makes the whole AND False.
